title: Match the Sieve

placement.where: After introducing sieving, the student picks the correct sieve for each solid-solid mixture, one at a time. The agent points at the next mixture or the right hole-size with highlight() and reacts to the emitted events; it never picks for the student.

studentControls.effect: Moves from the intro/demo into the walkthrough of the mixtures.
Picks that sieve for the mixture on screen. The correct one plays the sifting animation and marks the mixture solved; a wrong one shows a targeted hint.
Jumps to that mixture (only ones already reached or solved).
Replays the sifting animation for the solved mixture.
Moves between mixtures, or restarts the whole activity.

answerKey.note: 'none' (the 'Sieving Won't Work' card) is a distractor: it is wrong for all three, because each mixture is a solid-solid mix that a sieve CAN separate.

scientificFacts: Sieving separates a solid mixture whose pieces are different sizes.
The smaller pieces fall through the holes while the larger pieces stay on top.
The mesh has to match the mixture: holes small enough to trap the big pieces, but big enough to let the small pieces through.
A liquid mixture cannot be sieved - the liquid runs straight through, so you filter it instead.
